violations.text: At the time of inspection an allergen statement was not visible to customers in the self-service food area. PIC is to supply an allergen statement. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

At the time of inspection, the hot water of hand sink was turned off at the control valve below. Per the PIC hot water is turned off due to a constant dripping. Maintenance is to be conducted to ensure that the hot water is available as needed. Per the PIC, employee can turn on the hot water valve below the sink as needed when washing hands. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

Observed a working container used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. -Reviewed proper procedures with PIC regarding moving chemicals from bulk supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (QT) sanitizing solution for the (3-bay sink). New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

Dumpster was overflowing and several bags of trash was observed outside of dumpster at time of inspection. A storage area and enclosure for REFUSE, recyclables, or returnables shall be maintained free of unnecessary items, and clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026
At the time of inspection an allergen statement was not visible to customers in the self-service food area. PIC is to supply an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

At the time of inspection, the hot water of hand sink was turned off at the control valve below. Per the PIC there is a leak in pipes within wall fixture when hot water to hand sink is turned on and left on. Maintenance is to be conducted to ensure that the hot water is available as needed. Per the PIC, employee can turn on the hot water valve below the sink as needed when washing hands. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2025

Yogurt, boiled eggs and cream cheese was observed held at 43-50 degrees Fahrenheit in mini refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. -Based on time foods placed out for food service and temperature of refrigeration where bulk items are held PIC removed food items to the kitchen refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Observed several working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. -Reviewed proper procedures with PIC regarding moving chemicals from bulk supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Observed mini refrigerator located in the self-service area maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit and need of adjustment. - At the time of inspection employee was able to adjust temperature. - Temperature came down to 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Equipment and utensils must be sanitized after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Inspector demonstrated how to properly set up a sanitizing bay in the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023

The reach-in refrigerator in the kitchen was observed not reaching an adequate ambient temperature in a timely manner. Unit must not be used for TCS foods until serviced. The refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2023

Wood is used in the construction of 3-bay sink in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2023
